# feat(ml): GPU-first Phase 1 — DeviceReport + km.device()/use_device()

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `640832e347bc` — feat(ml): GPU-first Phase 1 — DeviceReport + km.device()/use_device()

**Body**:

Foundational layer for the GPU-first punch list at
`workspaces/kailash-ml-gpu-stack/04-validate/02-revised-stack.md`. Lands
items 2 and 7 from the 8-item list; items 3-6 (sklearn Array-API,
XGBoost/LightGBM OOM fallback, RL audit, UMAP/HDBSCAN adapters) build on
this foundation in a follow-up session.

Items shipped:

* **Item 1 — [rapids] extra**: confirmed already absent from
  packages/kailash-ml/pyproject.toml; no-op.

* **Item 2 — DeviceReport**: new frozen dataclass at
  kailash_ml/_device_report.py carrying per-call evidence (family,
  backend, device_string, precision, fallback_reason, array_api).
  Validates that "auto" values are BLOCKED at construction time — a
  runtime report MUST carry evidence, not intent. Helper
  `device_report_from_backend_info()` is the 90% convenience over a
  BackendInfo plus family label.

* **Item 7 — km.device() + km.use_device()**: top-level inspection
  helper and context manager at kailash_ml/__init__.py. km.device()
  returns the resolved BackendInfo without training anything;
  km.use_device(name) pins backend selection for the scope of the
  block. Contextvar-scoped so it is thread-safe and asyncio-safe.
  Unknown backends raise at pin time (not first use), and
  BackendUnavailable fires eagerly when the pinned backend is not
  present on the host — deterministic failure surface beats silent
  wrong-backend execution.

24 unit tests added (test_device_report.py) covering the validation
contract, log payload shape, and contextvar scope in asyncio. All pass
on CPU-only hosts.

Follow-up for next session: items 3 (sklearn Array-API allowlist), 4
(OOM fallback helper + XGBoost/LightGBM wiring), 5 (RL device routing
audit), 6 (CPU-native UMAP/HDBSCAN adapters), and 8 (per-family per-
backend regression tests). Each of items 3-6 will consume the
DeviceReport contract landed here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
